mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2013 payroll growth at 137,041 thousand persons indicates post-recession labor recovery
regime_hint: post-recession expansion with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months produce negative 20d forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above its 12-month trend, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and produces positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPI at 233.947 indicate near-zero real rates
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with moderate inflation
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods produce positive 60d forward returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals weakening consumer demand that reduces near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice equity valuations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2013 RSAFS at 425,881 million dollars shows consumer spending levels
failure_condition: Months with > 2% RSAFS declines produce negative 5d forward returns in < 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 7.0%, signaling stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2013 INDPRO at 100.082 and UNRATE at 7.2% indicate labor slack with modest production
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with labor market slack
failure_condition: Stagflation trigger conditions produce negative 20d forward returns in < 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 900,000 units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, signaling robust construction demand and wealth effects that drive positive equity returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on employment and consumption.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 900,000 units annualized AND GDPC1 quarterly growth > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:August 2013 HOUST at 917,000 units and July 2013 GDPC1 at 15,790.1 billion indicate housing recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Housing acceleration with positive GDP produces positive 60d forward returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing negative 20-day forward returns as investors reduce equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2013 UNRATE at 7.2% reflects ongoing labor market normalization
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes > 0.3pp produce negative 20d forward returns in < 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held constant below 0.15% for more than 6 consecutive months while inflation remains below 2.5%, the market interprets this as extended monetary accommodation, reducing equity risk premiums and producing positive 5-day forward returns on days when this regime is confirmed.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.15% for 6+ consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:October 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% reflects extended zero lower bound policy
regime_hint: extended zero lower bound with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Extended accommodation regime produces positive 5d forward returns in < 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains elevated above 7.0%, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market slack that reduces near-term earnings growth expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2013 INDPRO at 100.082 and UNRATE at 7.2% indicate production constraints with labor slack
failure_condition: Production declines with high unemployment produce negative 20d forward returns in < 53% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
